articles.title: Bihar: Pedestal Fan Installed Outside Jail Cell at Mahua Police Station To Beat Heatwave, Video Surfaces - LatestLY
articles.full_text:
Bihar: Pedestal Fan Installed Outside Jail Cell at Mahua Police Station To Beat Heatwave, Video Surfaces
A viral video shared on X by journalist Sachin Gupta shows a pedestal fan placed outside the lockup bars at Mahua Police Station in Bihar. Positioned in a corridor to blow air into the cells, the setup aims to relieve detainees during a severe heatwave. The clip sparked widespread online reactions over the unusual 'jugaad' cooling method.
A video highlighting a unique cooling arrangement at the Mahua Police Station in Bihar's Vaishali district has gone viral on social media, drawing widespread attention amidst a regional heatwave. Shared on X (formerly Twitter) by journalist Sachin Gupta, the video shows a pedestal-standing fan placed directly outside the bars of the police station's lockup cell. The fan was positioned in the open corridor to blow air through the iron grates, providing much-needed relief to the detainees inside as temperatures continue to soar. Accompanying the footage, the viral post's caption read, "Thanks to the Bihar government on behalf of all the people locked up in the police station lockup!" The clip has sparked a wave of reactions online, with many users noting the unconventional setup as a classic example of localised "jugaad" (resourceful hacking) to cope with the extreme summer heat.
